title: Will Taylor Swift Attend the 2026 Grammy Awards?
summary: Is Taylor Swift going to the Grammy Awards this year? What we know.
story: Fans want Taylor Swift to "Speak Now" on whether she'll be dancing in her best dress on the Grammy floor.
The 68th Annual Grammy Awards return to Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles on Sunday, and Swift has not publicly commented on if she'll be in attendance on Feb. 1.

Swift's last absence from the Grammys came in 2022, even though her record "Evermore" was nominated for album of the year. But in 2026, Swift is not up for any awards, which hasn't occurred since 2017. (Swift did not attend that award ceremony.)

Swift's most recent album, "Life of a Showgirl," was released on Oct. 3, 2025, outside of the 2026 Grammy eligibility period of Aug. 31, 2024 — Aug. 30, 2025. This means that while 2026 isn't her year, her 12th studio album could receive nominations for the 2027 Grammys.

Swift is known to the Grammys audience not just for her many wins — including four album of the year awards — but also for her energy. At previous ceremonies, Swift has been seen standing up and applauding her fellow artists and dancing to all the performances.
While the singer was up for six Grammys last year, her last win — sorry, wins — came in 2024, when she won both best pop vocal album and album of the year for "Midnights."
In total, Swift has received 58 nominations and won 14 Grammy awards over the course of her career.

title: Why Filming ‘The Pitt’ Next Door to ‘Abbott Elementary’ Was ‘Full Circle’ For Katherine LaNasa
summary: Katherine LaNasa talks getting cast on "The Pitt," what's ahead for her character, charge nurse Dana, on Season 2 and filming next to "Abbott Elementary."
story: When Katherine LaNasa walked onto the set for "The Pitt," one of the first people she saw was Lisa Ann Walter.
"I'm like, 'This is such a full circle moment,'" LaNasa tells TODAY.com. "And I think she looked at me like, 'Who is this chick?'"

"The Pitt," which stars LaNasa as its fearless leader and charge nurse Dana Evans, films on the Warner Bros. Studios lot in Burbank, California, right next door to the set of ABC's sitcom "Abbott Elementary."
Among the stars of "Abbott Elementary" is Walter, who plays the "no-nonsense," South Philly Italian American teacher Melissa Schemmenti.

Many fans have clocked that both Melissa and Dana epitomize their respective Pennsylvania regions, from the teacher's passion for the Philadelphia Eagles to Dana's strong Pittsburgh accent. In fact, one viewer posted on Threads the theory that the two characters could be cousins. "They mostly get along, but the Philly vs. Pittsburgh ribbing is constant and mutual," they wrote.
It even drew the attention of Walter.
"This is also in my head," she wrote. "Fun fact, we shoot our shows next door."

"God, I love Lisa Ann Walter," LaNasa says in response. "In fact, when I was trying to figure out how to do a Pennsylvania accent in general, I watched her on 'Abbott Elementary.'" (Appearing on TODAY Jan. 29, she also cited "Mare of Easttown" as inspiration for how she nailed the Pittsburgh, Mid-Atlantic dialect.)
But LaNasa says her connection to the actor goes way beyond "The Pitt."
Before the medical drama came along and earned her an Emmy award, LaNasa was in a "complete dry spell."
"I couldn't get arrested," she says, an idiom in show business that refers to continually being overlooked for roles and achievements.
She recalls some uplifting words she received from her friend Elaine Hendrix, who starred in "Dynasty" with LaNasa's husband, Grant Show.

"I was really upset about it in the few years before 'The Pitt.' I was having just a complete dry spell," LaNasa says. "And Elaine said, 'Just keep going. Look at Lisa Ann. She couldn't get arrested, and now she's on 'Abbott Elementary.'"

Walter and Hendrix, a famous best friend duo, both starred on "The Parent Trap" in 1998. LaNasa recalls Walter having "shows developed around her" before hitting her own dry spell, which eased with her casting in the hit ABC show.
"For her to drop down and then come back up, it's like, 'Oh, I have to remember these hills and peaks and valleys,'" LaNasa says.
"And the first day I got to work on ('The Pitt'), there was Lisa Ann Walter," she adds.
Both "The Pitt" and "Abbott Elementary" have received heaps of recognition at the Golden Globes, Emmy Awards and by fans.
The medical drama is currently airing its second season on HBO Max, with new episodes dropping every Thursday until the Season 2 finale on April 16.
Here's what's ahead for LaNasa's character looking to the rest of Season 2.
What's Ahead for Nurse Dana on 'The Pitt'?
After being assaulted by a patient in Season 1 of "The Pitt," Nurse Dana said in the finale that she wouldn't be returning to the emergency department.

But to the great joy of fans, LaNasa didn't go anywhere. Dana is seen back on shift when the show picked up 10 months after Season 1, on the Fourth of July. So far this season, which just aired  Episode 4 on Jan. 29, Dana has largely been training a new staff member, Emma (Laëtitia Hollard), and dealing with cases including the long overdue cast removal of a man experiencing homelessness and a baby Jane Doe who was found in the waiting room bathroom.
LaNasa says the nurse Dana we're encountering in Season 2 is largely the same as Season 1, just with a new daily sense of hypervigilance on the job.

"Now that she's back, I don't know that she's entirely ready, and I don't know that people really ever get over something like that," LaNasa says. "I think when people are assaulted... I think that sticks with them for a long time.

"I had an incident one time. I was assaulted by a guy on a bike. And (now), I never see a guy going by on a bike that I'm like, 'What's that guy on the bike doing?' And so I would imagine only 10 months later, one would still be reeling from that," she adds.
In the character's personal life, which viewers don't see on screen due to the show's real-time format, LaNasa says much is the same for Dana.
"I view her husband, Benji, as kind of a grounding force," LaNasa says. "And she has three daughters, and in my mind one of them is one that you always worry about."

"In my mind, the daughter struggled with her own sobriety ... Dana also has a granddaughter, so my own daughter (Eloise) plays Dana's granddaughter in my pictures at my desk," she adds with a laugh. "I've cast my own daughter as Dana's granddaughter."
As for what's ahead in the next 10-plus episode of Season 2, LaNasa teases the plot point she's most excited for fans to see unfold — and it includes Noah Wyle's character.
"There's conflict between Dana and Robby this year," she says. "That's all I'm going to tease. ... It's good. It's juicy."

title: Who Is Hosting the 2026 Grammys? All About Former 'Daily Show' Host Trevor Noah
summary: Trevor Noah will be hosting the 2026 Grammys! Learn all about the former host of "The Daily Show" and why he gets "nervous" hosting events.
story: Trevor Noah is taking his sixth, and final, spin as the host of the Grammys.
Earlier this year, Grammys executive producer Ben Winston confirmed this is the last time the South African comedian will be taking on the role.
“I am beyond thrilled to welcome Trevor Noah back to host the Grammys for his sixth, and sadly, final time,” Winston said in a statement, according to The Hollywood Reporter.

“He’s been the most phenomenal host of the show. He’s so smart, so funny, and such a true fan of the artists and music. His impact on the show has been truly spectacular, and we can’t wait to do it together one last time.”
Scroll below to learn more about Noah, his Grammys history and long career.
Trevor Noah Has Been Nominated for 4 Grammys
Noah is a four-time Grammy nominee.
He was nominated in 2020, 2024 and 2025 in the best comedy album category for his work in “Trevor Noah: Son of Patricia," "Trevor Noah: I Wish You Would" and "Trevor Noah: Where Was I."
He's also up for a Grammy in 2026 for his audio book "Into The Uncut Grass."
Trevor Was the Longtime Host of 'The Daily Show'
In 2014, Noah became the Senior International Correspondent for "The Daily Show." A year later, he succeeded Jon Stewart as host of the program.
During his first day on the job, Noah paid homage to Stewart in his opening monologue.
“Many people are part of the reason that I’m sitting here today, but above all, I would be remiss if I didn’t acknowledge one man, and that’s Jon Stewart,” he said.

“Thank you, Jon. Thank you for believing in me. I’m not quite sure what you saw in me, but I’ll work hard every day to find it, and I’ll make you not look like the crazy old dude who left his inheritance to some random kid from Africa.”
Noah ended up leaving "The Daily Show" in 2022 to pursue other opportunities.
"There’s another part of my life that I want to continue exploring," he said on the show when he announced his departure.
“The point is, I want to say thank you,” he added. “Because man, I never dreamed that I would be here.”
He's From South Africa
Noah was born in Johannesburg, South Africa in 1984. He wrote about his upbringing under apartheid in the memoir "Born a Crime."
Noah was, indeed, born a crime: His mother is Black and his father is Swiss-German, and at the time, it was illegal to have a mixed race relationship in South Africa.
In a 2016 interview with "Fresh Air," Noah called his mom, Patricia Nombuyiselo Noah, the "hero" of his memoir.
Trevor Noah Speaks Six Different Languages
In a 2016 interview with Fresh Air, Noah revealed he speaks “six” languages.
“I speak English, obviously; Afrikaans, which is a derivative of Dutch that we have in South Africa; and then I speak African languages,” he said.

“So I speak Zulu, I speak Xhosa, I speak Tswana and I speak Tsonga. So those are my languages at the core. And then I don’t claim German but I can have a conversation in it, so I’m trying to make that officially my seventh language, and then hopefully I can learn Spanish.”
Trevor Noah Is a Philanthropist
In 2018, Noah launched the Trevor Noah Foundation, which aims to "improve equitable access to quality education for underserved youth in South Africa," his official website states.
According to the organization, they have helped around 10,000 kids in South Africa and are committed to extending their footprint across all of Africa.
He's Always 'Nervous' to Host An Event or Do Standup Comedy
In a 2024 interview with People, Noah revealed just how hard it is to host an awards show or do standup comedy.
"I’ve never approached either an award show or a standup show without nerves," he said.
"I always care about what happens. I don’t take anything for granted, and I’m never sure that or how a joke will land. You just prepare. You hope for the best."

Noah said that "telling a joke is like jumping out of an airplane with a parachute," meaning, "you have an idea of where you would like to land, but when you actually get close to the ground, you might be a little bit off from where you intended to be."
"So, I just work my a-- off, try and think of the funniest things to say and ways to say them. The rest I leave to God," he explained.

title: From Collaborators to Spouses: A Timeline of Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco's Relationship
summary: Selena Gomez may have said "I love you" first, but her husband, Benny Blanco, certainly shows his love in countless other ways.
story: Selena Gomez may have said "I love you" first, but her husband, Benny Blanco, certainly shows his love in countless other ways.

Gomez and Blanco's love story did not arrive with a red carpet debut, it snuck up and had fans questioning how long they had been together. What began as a friendly collaboration between two longtime music insiders has evolved into one of pop culture's most unexpected and charming relationships.

Before beginning their romantic relationship, Gomez and Blanco worked together in the same creative circle, dating back to 2015. When they went public with their relationship in December 2023, fans traced back their connections to piece together their relationship timeline.
Now, over the years, the pair has made countless public appearances together and frequently post about their marriage online.
So, how did two musically gifted friends turn into husband and wife? Here’s a timeline of key moments in Gomez and Blanco’s relationship.
October 2015: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Collaborate for First Known Time
In 2015, Gomez released her second studio album, “Revival.” For that project, Blanco was credited as a producer on several songs, such as “Kill Em with Kindness” and “Same Old Love.”
February 2019: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Collaborate on ‘I Can’t Get Enough’
In 2019, Blanco and Gomez returned to the recording studio to work on the track “I Can’t Get Enough,” which also features Tainy and J Balvin.
In the music video, Blanco and the three artists dance around on a giant-sized bed.
August 2023: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Collaborate on ‘Single Soon’
In August 2023, Gomez released the song “Single Soon,” where she sings about the excitement that someone can feel when they get out of a relationship.
While teasing the song on Instagram, Gomez said she wrote the upbeat tune “a while back” and said her song was “perfect for the end of summer.”
Blanco has production credits on this song, as well.
December 2023: Selena Gomez Confirms She’s Dating Benny Blanco
In December 2023, Gomez confirmed that she and Blanco were dating when she liked and replied to a post about her relationship status.
Instagram account Popfactions wrote, “#SelenaGomez seemingly confirms that she is in a relationship.”
A few days later, Gomez responded in the comments with the single word: “Facts.”
Fans rejoiced when Gomez liked another Popfactions post that said she and Blanco were rumored to be dating.

As news started to heat up about a romance between the two, Gomez shared a black-and-white photo on her Instagram story of her snuggling up to a mystery man in a zebra-printed shirt that many eagle-eyed fans suspected was Blanco.
Then, shortly after sending the internet abuzz, Gomez and Blanco went Instagram official when the singer shared a sweet picture of them kissing within a larger photo carousel.

In the post, Gomez also shared some of her “favorite moments” from her time in New York. She included a photo of her attending a soirée with Taylor Swift and another picture of her and Blanco at what appeared to be a dinner party.
In that snap, Blanco had his arms wrapped around Gomez as she smiled for the camera.
December 2023: Selena Gomez Opens Up About Her Love Life
In an interview with Vogue México y Latinoamérica, published Dec. 21, 2023, Gomez opened up about her love life and revealed what she’s looking for in a partner.

“Honestly, I have to start being attracted to the right kind of people, because it’s a bit of both,” she said. “It’s very hard to find someone who can listen to you and care about you, but I know when it happens it will be great and you will want it to be healthy.”
In the interview, Gomez also said she hopes to become a mother one day, saying it’s “fundamental to her life’s purpose.”
January 2024: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Attend a Lakers Game Together

On Jan. 3, 2024, Gomez and Blanco were seen sitting courtside at a Los Angeles Lakers game. As the Lakers faced the Miami Heat, the couple were seen cuddling up next to each other and sharing several laughs.
At one point, Gomez was captured laying her head on Blanco’s shoulder, and at other moments, he was photographed kissing her hand and placing it hand on her knee.
January 2024: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Attend the Golden Globe Awards

Gomez posted a photo on her Instagram story of her and Blanco locking lips on Jan. 7, 2024 — the same night she attended the Golden Globe Awards. Decked out in a red dress, Gomez was seen pressing her lips into Blanco’s.
“I won,” she captioned the picture.

Gomez’s comment alluded to her defeat in the best performance by a female actor in a television series category. She was nominated for her role in “Only Murders in the Building,” but lost to Ayo Edebiri for “The Bear.”
March 2024: Selena Gomez Celebrates Benny Blanco’s Birthday
On March 8, 2024, Gomez celebrated Blanco’s birthday by sharing a few sweet pictures and videos of them together on Instagram.
In the post, Gomez shared a snap of her smooching Blanco, as well as a video of them kissing each other in the dark.

She captioned it, “Happy birthday baby! Your emotional endurance, positive disposition, unbelievable talent (that blows me away), undeniable humor and loving, kind heart absolutely kill me. I love you @itsbennyblanco.”
Blanco commented with several emoji, including the teary-eyed happy face emoji.
April 2024: Benny Blanco Recalls Falling ‘in Love’ With Selena Gomez
Blanco opened up about his relationship with Gomez in an interview with The Wall Street Journal, published April 25, 2024.
Noting that they had been friends for nearly a decade, Blanco said he didn’t immediately recognize the romantic spark between himself and Gomez — until one day, in the middle of a recording session.

“I was the last one to know,” he said. “It’s crazy how your partner could just be sitting there the whole time, right in front of your eyes, and you don’t even notice, and then you have that ‘Clueless’ moment where you’re like, ‘Wait, I’m in love.’”
In that same interview, the outlet reported Gomez and Blanco began officially dating in June 2023.
April 2024: Selena Gomez Supports Benny Blanco’s at His Cookbook Launch Party
In April 2024, Blanco released his book, “Open Wide: A Cookbook for Friends,” where he shares his best dinner party recipes.
To celebrate, Blanco threw a party featuring a cake in the shape of the book.
On Instagram, Gomez shared pics from the party. In one snap, she kissed his life-size cake, and in another, she posed for the camera while Blanco hugged her from behind.
“So proud of @itsbennyblanco — Open Wide is out now!!” she captioned the post.
May 2024: Benny Blanco Talks Winning Over Selena Gomez With Cooking

Blanco opened up about his relationship with Gomez while talking about “Open Wide: A Cookbook for Friends,” on TODAY With Hoda & Jenna in May 2024. Blanco recalled the time when he made a “perfect” steak dinner for Gomez.
While joking about the moment, he said he advises everyone to learn to cook to win over potential partners.

“Look at me, the only way I got where I am is by cooking food. Are you kidding me? That’s the best first date in the world, you cook up some food! How could anyone not love you if you’re making them a huge steak with potatoes and salad,” he said.
Blanco then talked about how it felt to have Gomez at his book party and what it’s like to have a supportive girlfriend by his side.
“I wake up every day and I look in the mirror and I’m like, ‘How did this happen?’ Until anyone figures it out, ‘Wee!’” he joked.
May 2024: Selena Gomez Says She Thought About Adopting a Child Before Dating Benny Blanco
In a Time interview published in May 2024, Gomez shared that after years of being single, she made a plan to become a parent before she and Blanco started dating.

“I was alone for five years, and I got really used to it. A lot of people are afraid of being alone and I probably tortured myself in my head for like two years being alone, and then I kind of accepted it,” she told the magazine. “Then I came up with my plan, which was I was going to adopt at 35 if I had not met anyone.”

Before they became a couple, Gomez shared that she asked Blanco if he had any friends he could set her up with. When he introduced her to his friend at a party, Gomez had the realization that she was actually interested in her friend, Blanco.
“It just happens when you least expect it,” she added.
July 2024: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Try Viral Couple’s Trend on TikTok

In a TikTok video posted July 12, 2024, Gomez and Blanco took part in a viral couples challenge, which consists of two people reacting to “Who’s mostly likely to?” prompts, such as “Who cleans the house?” (The answer: Blanco).

In the 30-second clip, the couple reacted to the prompts, disagreeing on some points — they both pointed to themselves for the prompt, “Who’s most likely to fall asleep during a movie?” — and agreed others applied equally.
But when the audio prompt asked, “Who said I love you first?” Gomez confidently pointed to herself and mouthed “me” at the camera.
Blanco, meanwhile, hugged her side and dropped a kiss on her outer arm.
November 2024: Selena Gomez Says Relationship With Benny Blanco Is the ‘Safest’ She Has Ever Felt

During an interview with The Hollywood Reporter touching on Gomez’s Disney Channel upbringing until now, the outlet pointed out that the “Emilia Pérez” star has been more public about her current romance compared to her past relationships.
“I guess this is the safest I’ve ever felt in one, and I see a future with this person,” Gomez said in the November 2024 story.
She added, “And when you put a little bit out there, people are not as hungry to hunt you down.”
Gomez said she still manages to keep many details of her relationship with Blanco private, explaining that “there’s so much of my relationship people don’t see, that’s just mine.”
December 2024: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Get Engaged

On Dec. 11, 2024, Gomez announced that she and Blanco were engaged when she shared photos of herself wearing an engagement ring on Instagram. In the post, she also included a snap of Blanco kissing her while she showed off her new ring.
Gomez captioned the pics, “forever begins now..”
Blanco jokingly commented, “Hey wait… that’s my wife,” and Gomez’s friend Swift wrote, “Yes I will be the flower girl.”
January 2025: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Celebrate New Year’s Day
In honor of New Year’s, Gomez shared a gallery of pictures on Instagram to kick off 2025.

The opening shot featured a paper plate with partially-eaten beans and the words “I LOVE YOU,” written in pen. The post included several shots with Blanco, a couple selfies and a picture of her half sister Gracie Elliot Teefey eating ice cream at Serendipity, the historic New York City diner of which Gomez is a partial owner.
“Happy new years,” she captioned her collection.
Blanco popped into the comments of Gomez’s post to write, “i love you more.”
September 2025: Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco Get Married
Gomez and Blanco were married Sept. 27, 2025. Both shared snapshots from the day less than 24 hours later. After nearly 10 months of engagement, the pair tied the knot in a ceremony in California.

In a series of faded Polaroid shots Gomez posted on Instagram, the actor donned a lace halter-neck wedding dress with a long white veil over her brunette bob. She carried a bouquet of baby’s breath alongside Blanco, who wore a classic black-and-white tuxedo.
She captioned the photo compilation, “9.27.25,” in reference to their wedding date.
Blanco shared several shots with his new wife on Instagram on Sept. 28, writing, “i married a real life disney princess.”
Gomez commented on the post, “I love you soooo much,” with a white heart emoji.

title: All About Charli XCX's Husband, George Daniel
summary: Charli XCX fell in love again and again with her husband, George Daniel. Meet the musician and artist who is married to Charli.
story: Charli XCX fell in love again and again with her husband, George Daniel.
The "Apple" singer has spent more than a decade turning her stories into pop anthems, but has remained relatively quiet on details of her personal life.

Fans have become increasingly curious about the musician's long-term partner and now husband, George Daniel — the drummer, producer and creative force behind one of the U.K.'s biggest bands, the 1975.
The pair are also frequent collaborators in the studio. From his role in the 1975 to his influence on Charli's music, here's what we know about the couple's life, and their work together.
He’s a Drummer for the Band The 1975
Daniel is a drummer for the English pop rock band The 1975. The band also consists of three other members: Matty Healy, Ross MacDonald and Adam Hann.
The 35-year-old and his bandmates became friends as teenagers when they were attending school together.
Per the New Yorker, they formed an “emo band that cycled through a series of emo names: Me and You Versus Them, Forever Drawing Six, the Slowdown, Drive Like I Do.”
While chatting with the publication, Daniel said of his relationship with Healy, “You love them more than anyone else in the world, and you cut them less slack than anyone else in the world.”
He Inspired One of Her Songs
While Charli XCX doesn’t share much about their relationship, she said, in a TikTok video, he inspired her song “Talk talk.”
The song dated back to a “specific moment” from their relationship when they “were not together” but were “very into each other.”

“We were at the NME Awards. We were both (sitting at) different tables, and we were, like, texting each other, but we weren’t hanging out,” she said. “But we were both looking over at each other — it was very like, one of us would look, and the other one would look away, and then vice versa.”
In her song “Talk talk,” the musician sings about following someone to the restroom. She said she actually did that in real life.

“I saw that he went to the bathroom, so I was like, ‘Oh, I’ll go to the bathroom, and maybe we’ll bump into each other, and then we won’t be, like, texting all the time,” she said. “But then I got halfway there, and I was like, this is insane and then I went and sat down again.”
Daniel and Charli XCX Have Collaborated on Songs
The British musicians have collaborated on several occasions over the years.

Per Rolling Stone UK, the pair first teamed up professionally in 2021 while working on the single “Spinning,” which featured Charli XCX, No Rome and The 1975. Since then, the couple has collaborated on several occasions.
While chatting with Rolling Stone UK, Charli XCX spoke about the unique dynamic they have while working in the studio as a couple.

“We want to impress each other, and we think we’re both really good, but I’m actually such a b---- in the studio with George,” she said. “Because we’re obviously so close, you lose everything in terms of the normal studio boundaries.”
All kidding aside, the musicians have found a way to make music together professionally and personally.
“We flow really well, and he leaves a lot of space for people to breathe and be them and do their process,” she said.
Charli XCX applauded her beau’s producing skills in a 2023 post on X.
“My boyfriend is really hot AND he’s a great producer! who says men can’t multitask???” she wrote.
His Career as a Musician and Drummer Dates Back to High School
In a 2016 interview with Coup de Main, Daniel said he started playing the drums in high school.
“I don’t really know why I wanted to start playing drums, I just did at school,” he said.

He started studying production also on a whim. “I took music tech, and then I still wasn’t really sure whether I had any kind of talent in it, and I did this film score arrangement for my GCSE exam and got full marks. I was just like, ‘What?’ I thought it was a mistake,” he said.
He continued to study production at college while the band was working on their own music.
Charli XCX applauded her beau’s producing skills in a 2023 post on X.
“My boyfriend is really hot AND he’s a great producer! who says men can’t multitask???” she wrote.
The Couple Confirmed Their Engagement in 2023
Daniel and Charli XCX announced their engagement in 2023 via a since-deleted Instagram post.
The Pair Had Two Wedding Ceremonies in the Summer of 2025

On July 19, 2025, Charli XCX and George Daniel first exchanged vows in a private, civil ceremony at Hackney Town Hall in London, Vogue reported at the time. The pair later shared pictures from the celebration to Charli XCX's Instagram page.

Nearly two months later, the couple had a more elaborate wedding in Sicily, Italy on Sept. 13, 2025, with several famous guests in attendance, according to Vogue. Charli XCX posted a carousel of pictures from the wedding festivities to her Instagram.
She captioned the post, "i wanna dance with george foreverrrr <3".

title: Michael Keaton Pays Tribute to Catherine O’Hara: ‘This One Hurts’
summary: Michael Keaton is honoring friend and former co-star Catherine O’Hara following news of her death at the age of 71.
story: Michael Keaton is honoring friend and co-star Catherine O’Hara following news of her death at the age of 71.

On Jan. 30, O’Hara’s manager confirmed to NBC News that the versatile actor, best known for her roles in “Home Alone,” “Schitt’s Creek,” “Beetlejuice” and more, died in her home after a brief illness.
Tributes have been pouring in from stars who shared the screen with O’Hara during her six decades-long career, including Keaton.

The two co-starred in 1988's “Beetlejuice” and reunited for the 2024 sequel, “Beetlejuice Beetlejuice.” They also appeared together in the 1994 workplace satire “The Paper” and the 2005 sports drama “Game 6.”

But as he wrote in his tribute, their friendship went beyond movie sets. Beside a photo of the two, Keaton said on Instagram, “We go back before the first Beetlejuice. She’s been my pretend wife, my pretend nemesis and my real life, true friend.”
He added, “This one hurts. Man am I gonna miss her.”
Keaton ended his message by seemingly writing that he was thinking of O’Hara’s husband, Robert W. “Bo” Welch III.

O’Hara and Welch met on the set of “Beetlejuice” when she played Delia Deetz and he worked behind the camera as a production designer. They married in 1992 and shared two sons together, Matthew and Luke.
Tim Burton also shared a group photo from “Beetlejuice Beetlejuice” set that included Keaton, Winona Ryder and Jenna Ortega.
“Catherine, I love you,” he wrote on Instagram. “This picture shows how much light you gave to all of us. You were a special part of my life and after life.”

Fellow “Beetlejuice Beetlejuice” star Justin Theroux posted a statement about O’Hara’s passing, as well. He uploaded a photo to Instagram of a director’s chair with O’Hara’s name written on it. His dog Kuma is also seen lying by the chair.
In the caption, he included a dove emoji and wrote, “Oh Catherine. You will be so so missed.”

title: Justin and Hailey Bieber's Relationship Timeline, From Their 1st Meeting at TODAY to Parenthood
summary: Justin and Hailey Bieber's relationship timeline starting from 2009 when they first met to 2018 when they got married after a whirlwind romance.
story: Justin and Hailey Bieber know invisible string theory all too well. Ten years after their first meeting backstage at TODAY in 2009, Hailey and Justin Bieber married and later became parents.

The two first met in 2009 at TODAY when Hailey Bieber’s father, Stephen Baldwin, introduced them to each other. At the time, Hailey Bieber was a preteen and Justin Bieber was a teenager. They later reconnected and married in 2018.
When asked by W Magazine in July how she felt about getting married at 21 years old, Hailey Bieber said she doesn’t regret it at all — she just wouldn’t tell someone else to do the same thing.
“I wouldn’t tell a 21-year-old in the chair right there, ‘I think you should get married.’ It’s really each individual’s experience,” she said.

The couple revealed they were expecting their first child in May 2024 they shared a video and series of photos of Hailey Bieber showing off her growing baby bump. Jack Blues Bieber was born in August 2024.

The couple has since kept Jack's face private, only including the back of his head in photos. Other than checking parenthood off the list, the Biebers have not been afraid to hit new career and relationship milestones, too.
Read on for a timeline of Justin and Hailey Bieber’s relationship.
2009: Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber meet for the first time
Justin and Hailey Bieber met for the first time in 2009 when Hailey’s father, Stephen Baldwin, introduced them to each other in the TODAY lobby.
December 2014: Hailey Bieber denies dating rumors

In 2014, rumors started to swirl that Justin and Hailey Bieber were seeing each other once they were spotted out together on multiple outings. However, in a December 2014 interview with E! News, Hailey Bieber said she and the “Baby” singer are just “good friends.”
“I’ve known him since I was so young — since I was like 13 — and we’ve just been good friends over the years,” she explained. “We have just stayed close and there’s nothing more to it than that.”
February 2016: Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber give insight into their relationship
In February 2016, Hailey Bieber gave some insight into her relationship with Justin Bieber when she told E! News that she and the pop star are “not an exclusive couple.”
“He’s about to go on tour. Relationships at this age are already complicated, but I don’t really like to talk about it because it’s between me and him,” she said.
In that same month, Justin Bieber did an interview with GQ and he told the magazine that he doesn’t want to rush things with Hailey Bieber.

“I don’t want to feel like the girl I love is an added responsibility,” he said. “I know that in the past, I’ve hurt people and said things that I didn’t mean to make them happy in the moment. So now I’m just more so looking at the future, making sure I’m not damaging them. What if Hailey ends up being the girl I’m gonna marry, right? If I rush into anything, if I damage her, then it’s always gonna be damaged.”
2016: Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber stop talking to each other
2016 was a tough year for the two. The couple stopped talking to each other shortly after they clarified their relationship status to E! News and GQ.
While thinking back on that time in her life, Hailey Bieber told Vogue in 2019 that her relationship with Justin Bieber had ended abruptly.
“Fizzled would not be the right word — it was more like a very dramatic excommunication,” she said. “There was a period where if I walked into a room, he would walk out.”
Hailey Bieber further reflected on this time period in a 2020 interview with Elle.

She said, “There was a time when our lives seemed to be going in very different directions. I actually think — now that I look back at it being married — that it was a good thing for the two of us, very healthy.”
2018: Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber get back in touch
It wasn’t until 2018 that Justin and Hailey Bieber started speaking to each other again.
In the interview with Elle, Hailey Bieber recalled their encounter.

“We ended up being at this church conference together in Miami, and it was the first time we’d seen each other in a while,” she said. “I remember we were hanging out and I was like, ‘Listen, I’m really, really happy for us to be friends again. I want us to always be cool and be friends.’ He was like, ‘Yeah, here’s the thing: We’re not going to be friends.’ And I was like, ‘Oh. Is that so?’
“I think we were both a little unsure about what was going on in the very beginning. It was familiar territory, but when a lot of time goes by, it’s like getting to know a new person,” she continued.
Hailey Bieber noted that she was impressed by how mature the “Boyfriend” singer had become.

“He had grown up so much. I was actually shocked. I think I had grown up a lot, too. He was somebody I’d always cared about so deeply and loved very deeply. Obviously, it took work and getting past things between the two of us, but it was all very worth it,” she said.
July 2018: Justin Bieber confirms he and Hailey got engaged
In a July 2018 Instagram post, Justin Bieber confirmed that he and Hailey Bieber had gotten engaged.
“You are the love of my life Hailey Baldwin and I wouldn’t want to spend it with anybody else,” he wrote.
September 2018: Alec Baldwin reveals Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber got married
At the 2018 Emmys, Alec Baldwin revealed his niece and Justin Bieber had married.
“They went off and got married,” he told “Access Hollywood“ when asked how “wild” a Baldwin wedding can get.
The couple had tied the knot in a New York City courthouse.
September 2019: Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber get married in a second wedding ceremony
In September 2019, the two got married again, but this time, they shared some photos from their wedding day on Instagram.
In a post, Justin Bieber shared a photo of him kissing his bride. He captioned it, “My bride is 🔥.”
According to People, the couple had 154 guests in attendance for their second wedding, held in South Carolina. Celebs who attended included Kylie Jenner, Usher and Jaden Smith.
March 2022: Hailey Bieber is hospitalized with a blood clot
In March 2022, Hailey Bieber was rushed to the hospital when she suffered a small blood clot to her brain.
“Immediately, I thought I was having a stroke,” she later said in a YouTube video posted on her channel, adding that it was “the scariest moment of my life.”

Hailey Bieber explained that she was diagnosed with a grade 5 PFO, which is a hole that exists in the heart, but usually closes after birth. “PFO is what the hole is called when it fails to close naturally after a baby is born,” per pennmedicine.org.
To remedy the PFO, Hailey Bieber said she underwent a PFO closure procedure, and once it was done, she started feeling great about her health.
May 2022: Justin Bieber opens up about married life
Justin Bieber got real about his marriage during a May 2022 appearance on “The Ebro Show.”
He said, “I remember when I first got married, I hit a little bit of an emotional breakdown because I thought marriage was going to fix all my problems and it didn’t.”

“It just kind of was a reflection of, like, ‘You’re a bit of a hypocrite, man. Like, you want your wife to do something that you’re not doing.’ And it’s like, I mean, it’s hard sometimes to look in the mirror and really have to realize, ‘Man, maybe you’re not the person that you necessarily thought that you were.’ And, you know, that’s just the result of trauma and life circumstances,” he said.
June 2022: Justin Bieber reveals he’s diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome
In June 2022, Justin Bieber revealed he was diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome.

According to the Mayo Clinic, Ramsay Hunt syndrome happens when a “shingles outbreak affects the facial nerve near one of your ears.” Some symptoms that can occur from Ramsay Hunt syndrome are a shingles rash, facial paralysis and hearing loss in the affected ear.

“Obviously, as you can probably see from my face, I have this syndrome called Ramsay Hunt syndrome,” Justin Bieber said in a video he shared on Instagram, which showed his lack of movement on one side of his face.
“As you can see this eye is not blinking. I can’t smile on this side of my face,” he added. “This nostril will not move. So there’s full paralysis on this side of my face.”
However, a few days after he shared that video, Justin Bieber gave his fans an update on his health and said he’s getting better.
“Each day has gotten better and through all of the discomfort I have found comfort in the one who designed me and knows me (Jesus),” he said.
March 2024: Hailey Bieber celebrates Justin Bieber’s 30th birthday

For her husband's 30th birthday, Hailey Bieber shared a number of photos and videos of the pop star over the years on Instagram, and wrote, “30!!!!!!!????!!????!!!😭 that was fast. words could never truly describe the beauty of who you are. Happy Birthday to you… love of my life, for life.”
May 2024: Hailey and Justin Bieber reveal they’re expecting their first child
Bieber party of two is becoming a party of three! The couple revealed May 9 that they’re expecting a baby, though they didn’t indicate the sex or when their first child is due.
They shared the news with a series of photos on Instagram in which Hailey Bieber is seen cradling a baby bump. The photos appear to be from a possible vow renewal ceremony.
On Aug. 23, Justin Bieber announced the arrival of his and Hailey Bieber’s baby. He posted the news alongside a photo of their newborn’s tiny foot.
“WELCOME HOME,” the singer captioned his post. “JACK BLUES BIEBER.”
August 2025: Jack's first birthday
With one year down, Hailey and Justin Bieber celebrated Jack Blue's first birthday on August 22, 2025. Hailey Bieber posted photos on Instagram of her and her little one exchanging hugs and smiles.
The couple also celebrated their firstborn with a camp-themed birthday party on August 25, 2025, called "Camp Jack."
September 2025: Justin shares family rules
Justin Bieber shared the Bieber family secret to living on Instagram.
Numbers one through ten emphasized words like worship, servanthood and generosity.
October 2025: Jack dresses as JB
Jack may have taken home the title for the best 2025 halloween costume.
The couple dressed Jack in Justin Bieber's "My World Tour" 2010s outfit. He was suited in white jeans and a purple sweatshirt with a white denim jacket over.
"Happy Halloween from JBB 🎃🎃🎃💜," Hailey Bieber wrote.
October 2025: Hailey reveals her favorite song on Justin's latest album
In an interview with BAZAAR, Hailey Bieber sat down to answer "burning questions." One of them being, which artist or album she was  obsessing over.
Justin Bieber released his album "Swag" in July 2025 and months later, Hailey Bieber was still hooked.
"Well, I think it goes without saying that 'Swag' is my album on repeat currently," Hailey Bieber said.
And as fans speculated, Hailey Bieber revealed her favorite track on Justin Bieber's July 2025 album.
"I love 'Daisies,' but I love the whole thing, so it’s hard for me to choose," she admitted.
November 2025: The Biebers dress as the Incredibles
For the Biebers, Halloween didn't stop in October.
Justin Bieber shared photos of him, Hailey and Jack posed in full "Incredibles" gear.
In the photos the power-couple is seen striking their best superhero poses and Hailey Bieber holds her real life version of baby Jack-Jack from the "Incredibles."
November 2025: Hailey debuts on the cover of GQ Men of the Year

The same year she sold her beauty brand Rhode in a deal worth $1 billion, Hailey Bieber starred in GQ’s Men of the Year 2025 edition. She opened up about balancing her public and private life as a family.
“We’re just taking it a day at a time,” she said.

“We both feel very protective of our son and I don’t think that’s ever going to change, but our life is our life and it is really public, so I think we’re just going to cross every bridge that we need to when we get there. But as of right now, I feel really comfortable about the way we are sharing things and not sharing things,” Hailey Bieber continued.

title: What Does Bad Bunny's 'Debí Tirar Más Fotos' Mean? Song Lyrics Translated
summary: Bad Bunny’s song “DtMF” topped charts and became a viral sensation following the release of his sixth studio album, “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.”
story: Bad Bunny’s song “DtMF” topped charts and became a viral sensation following the release of his sixth studio album, “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.” Both translate to “I Should’ve Taken More Photos.”
The song is about spending time with the people around you, documenting as much as possible before things change, and then acknowledging that they already have changed.

The title track also tackles themes of displacement of Puerto Ricans. In the song, he aches to collect memories with loved ones while also painting a picture of going out in Puerto Rico, with dominoes, drinks, family and friends.

This thread weaves throughout the “Debí Tirar Más Fotos” album, which honors Puerto Rican histories, incorporates various musical genres native to Puerto Rico and uplifts communities (while also providing beats you can dance your heart out to).
“DtMF” is nominated for song of the year and record of the year at the 2026 Grammys. The full album is also nominated for multiple awards, including album of the year.
With its sentimental tones, “DtMF” started a trend online where users add the song to nostalgic videos, looking back at heartfelt memories of loved ones, pets and experiences.

In January 2025, following the release of the album and the start of the “DtMF” trend, Bad Bunny posted a TikTok with the song playing and his face teary-eyed. The caption simply, “tik tok...,” referring to seeing all the emotional videos of people online using his audio.

The Puerto Rican singer, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, held a 30-day residency in “No Me Quiero Ir De Aquí,” which translates to “I Don’t Want to Leave Here,” in the capitol of the archipelago which boosted the economy, brought in tourism and celebrated the culture. Bad Bunny is also touring in South America and Europe.
During an with i-D magazine in September 2025, the singer spoke about his decision to not announce a tour in the U.S., partially due to the ongoing ICE raids in the country.
On top of his busy touring schedule, Bad Bunny is also the Apple Music Super Bowl LX Halftime Show performer.
Below, read the lyrics to "Debí Tirar Más Fotos," translated to English by TODAY.com.
'Debí Tirar Más Fotos' Lyrics Translated to English
Another beautiful sunset I see in San Juan
Enjoying all of those things that those that leave miss
Enjoying those kinds of nights that don’t happen anymore
That don’t happen anymore
But wanting to go back to the last time
That I looked in your eyes
And tell you the things I haven’t told you (you look like my crush, haha)
And take the picture that I didn’t take (Wow, you look so beautiful, let me take a photo)
Ey, my chest got scratched, I fell down
My heart, kicking me
Tell me baby, where you at?
To get there with RoRo, Julito, Krystal
Roy, Edgar, Seba, Óscar, Darnell y Big Jay, playing drums

title: Who's in the Cast of Season 4 of 'The White Lotus'?
summary: Who is in Season 4 of The White Lotus? Here's a list of the cast including what they've been in before, from Helena Bonham Carter to Chris Messina.
story: A new group of wealthy guests have made their reservations for what is sure to be a dramatic French vacation when "The White Lotus" returns for its fourth season.
The upcoming season of the HBO hit will feature the cast at a luxury property in France, after previous seasons were set in opulent locations in Thailand, Italy and Hawaii.
We also now know who will make up the new cast on the show, which has rotated in almost entirely new groups of actors every season under writer, director and executive producer Mike White.

They will have a tough act to follow, as seven actors from season three (Parker Posey, Jason Isaacs, Aimee Lou Wood, Carrie Coon, Sam Rockwell, Walton Goggins, Natasha Rothwell) all received Primetime Emmy nominations.

The plotline and characters for Season Four have not yet been revealed, but here's what we know about who will be featured in the newest season of rich hotel guests behaving badly and the frazzled staff who have to deal with them.
This story will be updated as more information about the cast emerges.
Helena Bonham Carter

It's nice to imagine the two-time Oscar nominee and nine-time Golden Globe nominee out in the sunshine on the French Riviera, as the British actor is best known for gloomy and gothic roles in movies like "Fight Club," "Sweeney Todd," the "Harry Potter" films, "Mary Shelley's Frankenstein," and a host of Tim Burton films.

Carter, 54, has also been honored by critics for her dramatic work in "The Wings of the Dove" and "The King's Speech." While she is best known for her film career, she has also done plenty of television work. She appeared on the Netflix hit "The Crown" and most recently is part of the British miniseries "Agatha Christie's Seven Dials," which was released on Jan. 15 on Netflix.
Chris Messina

Messina, 51, is best known for his work as Dr. Daniel Castellano on the comedy series "The Mindy Project" with Mindy Kaling, and he also starred as a sinister presence on the USA Network show "The Sinner" in 2020.

The actor is also no stranger to HBO projects, as he appeared as a detective on the acclaimed series "Sharp Objects" with Amy Adams in 2018. He also was part of the HBO hit "Six Feet Under" in 2005 and the drama "The Newsroom" from 2012 to 2014.
Marissa Long
This is the television debut for the model and actor.

"I’ll try and make this short and sweet But I am absolutely raw right now!!!" she wrote on Instagram about landing the role. "God is so good — I am in the middle of so many feelings and I’m unbelievably honored to be joining this with such incredible people."
Steve Coogan
A longtime comic actor, the British star is a two-time Oscar nominee for his 2013 movie "Philomena" with Judi Dench.
He's also known for his hilarious role as a doomed director in the comedy "Tropic Thunder," as well as the hit "Night at the Museum" films.

Coogan, 60, also starred in the cult 2002 film "24 Hour Party People" about the music scene in Manchester, England, and has been a staple of British television over the years with his Alan Partridge character.

Coogan also drew acclaim for his "The Trip" series of travelogues for the BBC between 2010-2020. His voice may sound familiar to fans of the animated "Despicable Me" movies as well as "The Minions" and "The Secret Life of Pets."
Caleb Jonte Edwards
Much like Marissa Long, this is the first major television project for Edwards, an Australian actor whose only previous credit is the Australian crime drama "Black Snow."
Alexander Ludwig
Best known for his role as Björn Ironside on the History Channel series "Vikings" from 2014-2020, Ludwig, 33, also had a memorable role in "The Hunger Games."
He also has appeared as a character in two of the "Bad Boys" films starring Will Smith and Martin Lawrence.
AJ Michalka
Michalka, 34, is best known for her work as a singer in the duo Aly & AJ with her older sister, Aly Michalka.
She also has been acting in films and movies since 2002, starting as a child actor with appearances on daytime soap operas and films like "The Lovely Bones."
Michalka additionally had a regular role on the ABC sitcom "The Goldbergs." In 2024, she popped up as one of the hidden performers on Season 12 of "The Masked Singer."

title: Legendary Actor Catherine O'Hara Dies at 71
summary: Catherine O'Hara, a legendary comedic actor known for "Beetlejuice," "Schitt's Creek" and more, has died at 71.
story: Catherine O'Hara, a legendary comedic actor whose career spanned 1980s hits like "Beetlejuice" to the current Apple TV sensation "The Studio," has died at 71, her manager confirmed to NBC News.
O'Hara died at home Jan. 30 after a brief illness, according to her agency.

The Canadian actor played wacky sculptor Delia Deetz in the 1988 hit "Beetlejuice" and starred as Macaulay Culkin's mom in the iconic 1990 hit "Home Alone." O'Hara reprised her role as Deetz in the 2024 sequel "Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice."

She also famously portrayed Cookie Fleck, the wife of Eugene Levy's character who keeps running into former boyfriends, in the 2000 comedy classic "Best In Show" about a prestigious competition in the vein of the Westminster dog show.

O'Hara re-teamed with Levy on the CBC comedy "Schitt's Creek" and won an Emmy for her role as eccentric former soap opera actor Moira Rose. The actor also won a Golden Globe in 2021 for her performance on "Schitt's Creek," which came into prominence when it was picked up by Netflix.
With her portrayal of the Rose family matriarch, O'Hara had fans cracking up with her unplaceable accent.

"The accent was kind of up to me," she told TODAY's Willie Geist during Sunday Sitdown in 2024. "The accents, I should say. When people try to imitate that character, the mistake most people make is to be consistent."
O'Hara was a 10-time Emmy nominee who took home a pair of awards for her acting in "Schitt's Creek" in 2020 and for her writing on the comedy show "SCTV Network 90" in 1982.

O'Hara was nominated for two Emmys in 2025, including one for outstanding supporting actress in a comedy series for her performance in the Apple TV hit "The Studio" with Seth Rogen. For her role as ex studio head Patty, she was also up for a 2026 Golden Globe Award, which were presented on Jan. 11.
She also showed off her versatility by earning an Emmy nomination for her dramatic work in the HBO series "The Last of Us," in which she played the therapist of Pedro Pascal's character, Joel Miller.
In the wake of her death, Pascal posted a photo of him and O'Hara on set in tribute.
"Oh, genius to be near you. Eternally grateful.  There is less light in my world, this lucky world that had you, will keep you, always," Pascal wrote in the caption.

She also appeared in the sequel “Home Alone 2: Lost in New York” and a host of mockumentaries by “Best In Show” director Christopher Guest like “Waiting for Guffman,” “A Mighty Wind” and “For Your Consideration.”
O'Hara grew up in Toronto as the sixth of seven children in a home filled with laughter.
"It was demanded more than encouraged in our home," she said on TODAY. "When you have that many people at the table, you better have something at least funny to say if not interesting."
When she was a teen, her brother was in a theater troupe with future "Saturday Night Live" legend Gilda Radner, who would come to O'Hara's home for dinners.

"She had my parents improv-ing and doing bits, too, and playing improv games," O'Hara said of Radner, who died in 1989. "She let all of us see that regular people that you know could actually be in show business and actually have a job doing this and get paid for doing this."

Radner inspired O'Hara to join the Second City comedy troupe at 18 years old. She came to prominence as a performer on "SCTV," a Canadian sketch comedy show that also featured legends like Levy, John Candy, Martin Short, Harold Ramis and Rick Moranis.
O’Hara is survived by her husband, Bo Welch, and sons Matthew and Luke, as well as siblings Michael O’Hara, Mary Margaret O’Hara, Maureen Jolley, Marcus O‘Hara, Tom O’Hara and Patricia Wallice.
A private celebration of life will be held by her family.

title: Macaulay Culkin Reacts to Catherine O’Hara’s Death: ‘Mama. I Thought We Had Time’
summary: Macaulay Culkin is mourning the death of Catherine O’Hara, who played his mother in the “Home Alone,” as well as its sequel, “Home Alone 2: Los in New York.”
story: Macaulay Culkin is mourning the death of Catherine O’Hara, who played his mother in the 1990 smash comedy “Home Alone” and its 1992 sequel, “Home Alone 2: Lost in New York.”
O’Hara died at the age of 71, her manager confirmed to NBC News. A cause of death has not been indicated.
“Mama. I thought we had time. I wanted more,” Culkin wrote on Instagram Jan. 30. “I wanted to sit in a chair next to you. I heard you. But I had so much more to say. I love you. I’ll see you later.

The pair had crossed paths in the years after “Home Alone.” In 2023, O’Hara spoke when Culkin received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, complimenting his work playing a young child whose parents accidentally left him behind while on a family trip in the first film, a theme that would recur in the sequel.
“Thank you for including me, your fake mom who left you home alone not once, but twice, to share in this happy occasion. I’m so proud of you,” she said.

“I know you worked really hard. I know you did,” she continued. “But you made acting look like the most natural thing in the world to do. It really was as if we had ambushed the home of this real little boy named Kevin to make a movie and he just went along with it for the fun of it. It’s the dearest thing.”

She has also recalled that she has been mistaken for being Culkin’s real mother, including at the Walk of Fame ceremony when he was asked to take some photos with Culkin and actor Natasha Lyonne, who was also on hand.

“His mother was not able to make it at that time. But we’re all posing, posing, posing,” she told TODAY’s Willie Geist during a 2024 Sunday Sitdown. “And then — now Natasha, now so-and-so. ‘OK, now just you and the mother.’ And I walk away and he said, ‘Just with the mother.’
“Then he puts his arm around, he goes, ‘Oh, no, my mom wasn’t able to make it,’” she continued. “‘Well then, I’m honored to be here. Thank you.’”
The “Home Alone” role would stick with O’Hara throughout her career.
“A child came up to me, like, a little 8-year-old, in a mall,” she told Willie.
“’Are you Kevin’s mom?’ I said, ‘Well, yeah, I played Kevin’s mom in a movie, yes.’ ‘Why did you leave him?’ ‘Sorry. It was in the script.’”

title: Bruno Mars and Rosé's 'APT.' Is a Hit, But What Does the Song Mean? Lyrics Explained
summary: What does "APT" mean? A Korean expert explains the song's meaning.
story: "APT.," one the biggest hits of the year by Rosé and Bruno Mars certainly has the potential to win big at the Grammys on Feb. 1.
The song is up for three nominations: Song of the year, record of the year and best pop duo/group performance. Bruno Mars and Rosé are also among the night's performers.
The song, released in 2024, brings together two forces in the music world: Many time Grammy-winner Bruno Mars and Rosé, a member of the K-pop girl group Blackpink.
Jae-Ha Kim, a journalist who specializes in Korean culture, says the song's cross-cultural appeal is no surprise.

"The entire song is an earworm! It has a catchy beat, English lyrics that are easy to sing along with, and two giant pop stars. Rosé and Bruno Mars’ voices blend so well together and they exude fun in the accompanying music video, which is really refreshing in its simplicity. It was also helpful that both artists have huge fanbases that supported the song from the get-go, which made the song impossible for the general public to ignore," she told TODAY.com in a statement.
What does the K-pop song mean? Here's what to know.
What Does 'APT.' Mean?
According to Kim, the title of the song, and the recurring word in Korean that pops up throughout the song, is the word for apartment.
"But Koreans don’t say ‘apartment.’ They say apateu/아파트(pronounded ah-pah-teu). Apateu is a word Koreans have used for decades," Kim said.
Fans of K-dramas might have also been familiar with this word before the song came around.

"In the 1980s, having a modern apartment was a status symbol," Kim continued. "K-drama viewers may recall hearing the word in shows, where the characters talk about wanting to move out of their old house into a new apateu."
What Rosé Has Said About the Lyrics’ Meaning
The lyrics have a double meaning. "Apateu" is also a popular drinking game in Korea. "In Korea, it’s called the Apartment Game," Kim said.
During an appearance on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon," Rosé said the song was inspired by turning the legal drinking age in Korea and playing APT with Blackpink.

“I remember when I turned 20, my Blackpink — the girls in the group, they got like, all the alcohol we could get. We were like, ‘We’re gonna drink tonight,” she told Fallon. “We’d play drinking games and ‘APT’ was one of them.”
The word "apateu" is repeated during the drinking game.
She explained how she brought the concept to the studio in an interview with Vogue.
"'Basically, we were hanging out in the studio and I was like, 'How about I teach you guys some Korean drinking games,' and then I started doing it," she said.
Rosé said her team was "obsessed" with the game and she decided to write a song about it. She started to second-guess herself soon after.

“I remember going home kind of freaked out. I was like, ‘Is this OK that I’ve written a song about a drinking game? Is this too unserious?’ And so I got all of the producers and songwriters to delete the song off of their phones.”
After showing it around to a few people, she said everyone was "so obsessed" with the song that she decided to bring it back to life and finish it.

title: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi Reveal ‘Wuthering Heights’ Director Made Shrines of Them In Their Dressing Rooms
summary: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi have a dynamic relationship on and off the screen.
story: Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi have a dynamic relationship on and off the screen.
The actors, who bonded over their Australian roots, co-star in the highly anticipated film “Wuthering Heights,” directed by Emerald Fennell.
Based on Emily Brontë's 1847 novel, the story is set in the late 18th and early 19th century and follows the heartbreaking and destructive romance between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw.

Committed to the brooding storyline, Robbie, 35, and Elordi, 27, revealed to TODAY correspondent Kaylee Hartung that their director made sure they worked on cultivating their romance between their characters.

“At the studio, we both had a dressing room. Emerald went into our dressing rooms and made a shrine of each other in our dressing rooms,” Robbie shared, with Elordi calling the move “funny” and “fantastic.”

Robbie added, “It was so unhinged and so hilarious. And she put, like, locks of hair and pictures of us, candles and made a full shrine... Mine was a Jacob shrine. He had a Margot shrine. It was so funny.”
The “Barbie” star explained that Fennell “set the bar” for how intense they should take their character’s romance.
“And then we just kept trying to meet the bar,” she said.
The Oscar nominated stars’ press tour has been filled with praise for each other, including Robbie telling Fandango that she became “codependent” on Elordi during the filming.
“I love everyone so much and I’m always that person who’s so devastated when a job’s over and I never want it to end,” she said. “I think I developed that quite quickly with Jacob, too.”
Elordi said during that interview that they had ⁠⁠“a mutual obsession.”

“If you have the opportunity to share a film set with Margot Robbie, you’re going to make sure you’re within 5 to 10 meters at all times, watching how she drinks tea, how she eats her food. She’s an elite actor,” he added. ⁠
Speaking with Hartung, Robbie said she “can’t imagine” doing the film with anyone other than Elordi.

“It would’ve been really difficult to do this role and play this relationship with someone who didn’t approach work in a same way that I do and that I didn’t genuinely get along with,” she said. “We both work hard and we both love what we do.”
The adoration is mutual for Elordi, who shared that he’s been a fan of Robbie since her work in the Australian soap opera “Neighbours.” Robbie was only 17 when she made her debut on the drama.

“I wanted to act so I had a hit list of every working Australian actor on my wall so I could find their agents and things that they’d been in,” Elordi said, before adding that seeing Robbie in “The Wolf of Wall Street” was when he knew she became “a global phenomenon.” “You came out in that film and something changed in the world, in my world.”
Both now Oscar-nominated actors, Elordi most recently for his role in “Frankenstein,” they said they find it “surreal” how far they’ve come.
“It’s like a silly big dream, you know?” Elordi said.

“It’s just kind of crazy that we’re here now in the middle of Hollywood doing this big Hollywood film,” Robbie added. “He’s from Brisbane, and I’m from the Gold Coast. Growing up, Hollywood feels so far away and this unattainable thing that you’re going to make it to. It’s kinda surreal.”
“Wuthering Heights” arrives in theaters on Feb. 13.

title: Who Is Alex Warren's Wife? All About Kouvr Annon, the Woman Who Inspired 'Ordinary'
summary: Who is Alex Warren's wife, Kouvr Annon? About their love story including when they met, how she inspired Ordinary, who introduced them and where they are now.
story: Alex Warren sings that his world was “black and white” until he met one not-so-ordinary woman in the hit song “Ordinary.”
Warren married his wife, Kouvr Annon, in 2024, and their relationship played out on social media for years before then. The two were both part of Hype House, a collective of social media influencers.
Annon has been by Warren’s side as they've skyrocketed to fame over the past eight years, from when they were sleeping in cars in California to Warren’s 2026 Grammy nomination.
Here’s what to know about Annon and their relationship.
She’s Originally from Hawaii
Annon is originally from Hawaii and graduated in 2018, according to a TikTok she made with Warren outside her high school.
In another TikTok, Warren and Annon surprise her family after she was gone for three years.
They Met in 2018 Through a Mutual Friend
Warren said, in a 2025 interview with the BBC, they were introduced through a mutual friend and connected on Snapchat.
“We just clicked right away. I felt I could tell her everything, just after our first conversation,” he said.
Four months later, according to the publication, Anon left Hawaii and moved to California to be with Warren. At the time, he was sleeping in a car, and she joined him.
They Were Part of Hype House
The couple posted videos of their relationship online and rose to fame. Warren, in 2019, co-founded Hype House, a group of creators and influencers living together under one roof.
The couple left in 2022 and went on to pursue each of their independent dreams. For Warren, that was songwriting.
Both remain influencers with huge followings. Annon, 25, has 16.3 million followers and documents her life, including her new horse, hobbies like knitting and interior design.
She’s Open About Her Health and Fertility Issues
Annon is also open about her Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) and fertility difficulties on her page, as well as her desire to be a mom.
The Couple Married in 2024

Warren kept all of social media on edge for when he would pop the question because of his videos joking about engagement. One of the more popular videos being one where he fake proposed for three years, so Annon would fail to expect the real proposal.
Warren finally got down on one knee for the real proposal on Dec. 23, 2022.
“I’m marrying my best friend ❤️💍,” Annon wrote on Instagram.
The couple got married in a fairy tale garden on June 22, 2024, according to People.
"Getting married is extremely important to me," Warren told People. "When I met Kouvr and the way she made me feel, I never wanted to let go of that feeling."

The ceremony was filled with easter eggs from the couple's past.  The couple was officiated by the man who took them in when the couple was sleeping in a car. Warren's late parents were also saved a seat at the ceremony and symbolized in his suit sunflower lining, for his mother, and pelican print vest in honor of his father.
“It felt so important to us to acknowledge that they were unable to be there in person but that we included them in our special day,” Annon also told People.
She Inspired the Song ‘Ordinary’
What followed was the birth of Warren’s hit song "Ordinary,” released in 2025.

“This song is now number 1 everywhere and I can’t help but smile to know it’s about you,” Warren wrote on Instagram. “It also reminds me that we’ve been together this long and I’ve never seen you run full speed.”
Warren also spoke to the music publication HITS about how rare he thinks it is to find true love today.
"We wrote ‘Ordinary’ about how love is falling out, but we found something crazy,” he said.
In a TikTok, Warren playfully joked with Annon about how she didn't know the lyrics.
“I thought the lyrics said, ‘And I’ll my life I’ll do ya,’” she said in the video.
“I thought you were talking about getting freaky, and I was, like, ‘Damn, my mom listens to this,’” she added.
The real lyrics go, “Oh, my life, how do ya?/ Breathe and take my breath away?”
Warren is now up for best new artist at the 2026 Grammys.
“Married to the best new artist…” Annon’s Instagram bio reads.

title: Kylie Kelce Has a Coy Answer to Question About Travis and Taylor’s Wedding on TODAY
summary: Kylie Kelce said she doesn't know when Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift will get married, even when TODAY's Savannah Guthrie pressed her on the matter.
story: Will Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married in 2026? If Kylie Kelce knows, she’s not letting on.

Kelce, who’s married to Jason Kelce and is sister-in-law to Travis Kelce, did her best to stay mum when she was asked about a possible wedding on TODAY. Kelce appeared on TODAY as a partner to Dove and exclusively revealed its Super Bowl spot.
“Do we think there’s a wedding this year?” Savannah Guthrie asked her Jan. 30 on TODAY.
“Oh, I don’t know,” Kelce replied with a shrug.
“OK. Do you really not know?” Savannah then asked.
“I have no idea,” Kelce responded. Then she grinned and laughed.
“You’re not going to lie,” Savannah said, in a nod to the name of Kelce’s podcast.
“Yes,” Kelce said, prompting Savannah to laugh. “I’m not going to lie.”
“You’re not going to get yourself in trouble, either, I know,” Savannah said.
“Never,” Kelce said. “We’re so excited to celebrate, though. We can’t wait.”
Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement in August, sending Swifties into a tizzy. Since then, the couple have not revealed any specifics about their wedding.
Swift, however, did say Travis Kelce popped the question shortly after she appeared on the “New Heights” podcast he hosts with his brother.
“He was top secret about this,” she said during an October appearance on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon.”
“And I really appreciate that. But then afterward I kind of realized, he’s walking around sort of nervous about the podcast.

“He’s like, ‘My heart is racing. I just really want — I know how much this means to you. I really want this to be what you hoped it would be,’ because we’d been talking about doing the podcast and announcing the album (‘The Life of a Showgirl’). He’s like, ‘My heart’s racing,’ and I’ve never seen this dude nervous ever. He’s not a nervous person.”

title: What Is a Ward? Explaining Sophie's Parentage, Social Status in 'Bridgerton' Season 4
summary: Sophie is described as being a ward in "Bridgerton" Season 4. What does that mean? Who are her parents? Her social status in Season 4 explained.
story: "Bridgerton" Season 4 has a new romantic lead who holds a unique place in London society.

Sophie Baek, played by Yerin Ha, is first introduced as the lady in silver, a woman who captivates our hero, Benedict (Luke Thompson), at the annual Bridgerton masquerade ball. But after a charged private conversation, she dashes off like Cinderella. Later, viewers find out why: She's a maid.

It's more complicated than that. In Episode 2, a series of flashbacks reveal the complicated circumstances of Sophie's place in society — and why it leaves her with guard constantly up, Ha tells TODAY.com.

The flashbacks begin with a young Sophie being raised by the Earl of Penwood, whom she calls "father." In the first scene, she stands outside with the house's staff as they await the arrival of the new Lady Penwood, Araminta Gun (Katie Leung), and her two daughters from a previous marriage. Sophie expresses her excitement at meeting her "new mother," but she's quickly chided by a servant to not call Lord Penwood father in front of his new wife.
When Araminta is introduced to Sophie for the first time, the earl calls Sophie, "my ward," as Araminta looks at him in shock.
What Is a Ward?
Today, ward is a legal term that refers to a minor placed under the protection of a court or court-appointed guardian.
In the Regency era, the term would more generally refer to any child requiring the protection of a guardian rather than a parent.

By calling her his ward, the Earl of Penwood claims responsibility for Sophie without claiming her as his own. But the flashbacks show that the earl raised her like nobility, providing dance lessons and tutoring for Sophie to complete with Lady Penwood's daughters, Rosamund and Posey.
"I will always make sure you are taken care of," he promises her after she asked about the identity of her mother.
After his death, Lady Penwood takes a different approach to caring for Sophie.
As Sophie mourns her father at his gravesite, Araminta reveals the truth about Sophie's parents.
Who Are Sophie's Parents?

Sophie is indeed the daughter of the Earl of Penwood. But as an illegitimate child, meaning she was born to two people who were not married, she carries little social status, and in fact, her very existence invokes scandal.
After the earl's death, Araminta finally tells Sophie the truth about her mother.
Sophie's mother was a maid who became the earl's mistress.
"She died when you were very small with no title, no money, no dignity and no husband," Araminta says, before claiming that Sophie was not included in the earl's will.
Araminta then reveals she will "take care" of Sophie by having her work as a maid in the Penwood house.
"But I am the daughter of a nobleman," Sophie protests.
“Being a maid is the best you can hope for. It will make you invisible,” Araminta says. “You will be protected this way. As long as you do not allow yourself to dream of more than your station.”
How Does the TV Show Change Sophie's Backstory?
While her situation in life is tragic, it's even more bleak in the original book, Julia Quinn's "An Offer From a Gentleman."
"Everyone knew that Sophie Beckett was a bastard," reads the book's opening line.

In the book, the earl does provide shelter for Sophie, but not much else. The prologue reveals that they would only see each other "a few times a year" and that Sophie was well aware of her illegitimate status, calling the earl simply, "my lord," rather than father.
Showrunner Jess Brownell tells TODAY.com that the change was made to "humanize" the people who raised her.
"There's a bit more distance and coldness between the earl and Sophie in the book, and on screen, it felt too bleak to have her father not acknowledge her," Brownell says.
It also provided a "dramatic payoff" for Sophie to discover the truth of her status.
"It actually, for us, created a bigger dramatic payoff for Sophie to have believed, truly believed, that her father loved her, and then have her stepmother try to deny that reality," Brownell says.
"So that's something that really plays into Sophie's character and into her approach with Benedict, actually."

title: 'Bridgerton' Star Yerin Ha Unpacks Sophie's Reaction to Benedict's Offer
summary: Why does Sophie run away from Benedict's offer to be his mistress in Season 4, Part 1 of Bridgerton?
story: Warning: This article contains major spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1.
"Bridgerton" Season 4 lived up to the title of the Julia Quinn book that inspired it in the final moments of Part 1.

Across the season's first four episodes, which hit Netflix Jan. 29, second son Benedict Bridgerton tries to hunt down the lady in silver, a woman he had a romantic first meeting with at his mother's masquerade ball. But in true Cinderella story fashion, she's right under his nose the entire time.

The lady in silver is actually a maid named Sophie Baek, played by newcomer Yerin Ha. She's the illegimate daughter of the late nobleman the Earl of Penwood, and while she was originally raised as his ward, after his death, she forced to serve his wife and stepdaughters.

But she's expelled from her home once Lady Araminta Gun finds out about her masquerade ball adventure, leading her to seek work out in the countryside. And through a series of coincidences, she ends up needing to find shelter alongside a wounded Benedict, who, of course, doesn't recognize her.

While he's still looking for the owner of the glove that was left behind by the lady in silver, he also finds himself drawn to Sophie as she stays by his side in the country, resulting in a sweet lakeside kiss.
In Episode 4, Sophie returns to London with him and is hired by his mother to work at Bridgerton House, further complicating their chemistry.

In the final moments of the Part 1, they run into each other on the stairs and give into a passionate, steamy kiss, set to an orchestral cover of Olivia Rodrigo's "Bad Idea Right?" — until Benedict asks a question that changes everything.
More about 'Bridgerton' Season 4

‘Bridgerton’ Season 4 Is a ‘Cinderella’ Re-Telling: All the Twists, References and Easter Eggs‘Bridgerton’ Season 4, Part 1, Ending: What This Character’s Surprise Reappearance Means for Francesca‘Bridgerton’ Showrunner on How Season 4 Will Embrace Benedict’s Sexuality
‘Bridgerton’ Season 4 Is a ‘Cinderella’ Re-Telling: All the Twists, References and Easter Eggs
‘Bridgerton’ Season 4, Part 1, Ending: What This Character’s Surprise Reappearance Means for Francesca
‘Bridgerton’ Showrunner on How Season 4 Will Embrace Benedict’s Sexuality
What Did Benedict Ask Sophie?
At the end of "Bridgerton" Season 4, Part 1, Benedict and Sophie give into their attraction after trying to stave it off once Sophie became an employee of the Bridgerton family.
After a passionate kiss, Benedict starts to confess his feelings.

"If you wish me to leave, I will, but the truth is, I stay away because you consume me," he tells her. "My eyes search for you in every room I enter. My heart beats when you are near. The reality of you has become more tantalizing than any fantasy ever could be, and one I cannot live without."
Sophie appears moved, and whispers Benedict's name in response.
"I meant it when I said you deserve better, and I am determined to give it to you and more," he continues.
"Sophie ... be my mistress," he concludes.
The music abruptly stops, and Sophie looks crestfallen by the titular "Offer from a Gentleman," which doubles as the name of the episode. She runs off, wordless.
Why Is Sophie So Hurt by Benedict's Offer?
While Sophie says nothing in response to Benedict's proposition, her face says it all.
Yerin Ha, who plays Sophie, tells TODAY.com that in that moment, her character "feels like she's let her guard down too much, and it's a reminder of exactly why she has it up."

Ha says the moment takes Sophie back to her childhood, when she was forced from a life of comfort and love with her father into that of servitude. After her father's death, Araminta tells Sophie at the earl's grave that she was not included in his will.
"My father loved me," Sophie says.
Araminta then tells her that Sophie's mother was a maid who "let herself become a mistress" and died without a husband, dignity or a title.
"Do not worry. I will take care of you now. I will feed you, clothe you, and keep a roof over your head. You will work as a maid," Araminta says.
Sophie protests that she is the daughter of a nobleman, before Araminta reminds her that she is illegitimate.
"Being a maid is the best you can hope for. It will make you invisible," Araminta says. "You will be protected this way. As long as you do not allow yourself to dream of more than your station."
Benedict professing his affection but asking her only to be his mistress stirs up the lingering hurt Sophie feels at being reduced to a maid, Ha explains.
"It's that mantra that Araminta always tells her, 'Know your place.' And I think that kind of kicks into gear," Ha says.
But even if Benedict did propose marriage, as would be expected for a noblewoman, Ha argues her character would still feel disappointed.

"Even if he did say, 'Be my wife,' I think almost in a way, she would maybe react the same, because it’s like, how do people from two different worlds coexist together when society doesn’t want them to?" she says.
"So yeah, I think that was what was going through my mind," Ha adds. "And just how passionately he delivered that."
"And how hot the staircase was," Thompson chimes in of filming the (literally) steamy scene.
"They built this new set and it was 15 stories high but there was like loads of candles and obviously all the heat rises, so it was boiling, and you nearly caught fire," Thompson says.
"My hair almost caught fire," Ha confirms with a laugh.
"That's what she was thinking," Thompson concludes.
"Bridgerton" Season 4 will conclude with four more episodes dropping on Netflix Feb. 26.
